items.docs:
Activates (or deactivates) a workflow in a given environment. Read more in the [docs](https://docs.knock.app/concepts/workflows#workflow-status).

Note: This immediately enables or disables a workflow in a given environment without needing to go through environment promotion.

items.docs:
Renders a template preview, without requiring a template to be persisted within Knock.
This is useful for previewing templates in isolation, without the need to use a workflow.

For email templates, you can optionally specify a layout by key or provide inline layout content.

items.docs: Retrieve a single commit by its ID.

params.name: id
params.description: The target commit ID to promote to the subsequent environment.

items.docs: Promotes one change to the subsequent environment.

params.name: to_environment
params.description:
A slug of the target environment to which you want to promote all changes from its directly preceding environment.

For example, if you have three environments “development”, “staging”, and “production” (in that order), setting this param to “production” will promote all commits not currently in production from staging.

When this param is set to `"development"`, the `"branch"` param must be provided.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of the branch to promote all changes from.

params.name: resource_type
params.description: Filter commits to promote by resource type(s). Accepts a single type or array of types. Can be combined with resource_id to filter for specific resources.

params.name: resource_id
params.description: Filter commits to promote by resource identifier. Must be used together with resource_type.

items.docs: Promote all changes across all resources to the target environment from its preceding environment.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

params.name: promoted
params.description: Whether to show commits in the given environment that have not been promoted to the subsequent environment (false) or commits which have been promoted (true).

params.name: resource_type
params.description: Filter commits by resource type(s). Accepts a single type or array of types. Can be combined with resource_id to filter for specific resources.

params.name: resource_id
params.description: Filter commits by resource identifier. Must be used together with resource_type. For most resources, this will be the resource key. In the case of translations, this will be the locale code and namespace, separated by a `/`. For example, `en/courses` or `en`.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of entries to fetch per-page.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of commits in a given environment. The commits are ordered from most recent first.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

params.name: commit_message
params.description: An optional message to include in a commit.

params.name: resource_type
params.description: Filter changes to commit by resource type(s). Accepts a single type or array of types. Can be combined with resource_id to filter for specific resources.

params.name: resource_id
params.description: Filter changes to commit by resource identifier. Must be used together with resource_type.

items.docs: Commit all changes across all resources in the development environment.

params.name: message_type_key
params.description: The key of the message type to validate.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

items.docs:
Validates a message type payload without persisting it.

Note: this endpoint only operates on message types in the `development` environment.

params.name: message_type_key
params.description: The key of the message type to retrieve.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

params.name: annotate
params.description: Whether to annotate the resource. Only used in the Knock CLI.

params.name: hide_uncommitted_changes
params.description: Whether to hide uncommitted changes. When true, only committed changes will be returned. When false, both committed and uncommitted changes will be returned.

items.docs: Retrieve a message type by its key, in a given environment.

params.name: message_type_key
params.description: The key of the message type to upsert.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

params.name: annotate
params.description: Whether to annotate the resource. Only used in the Knock CLI.

params.name: force
params.description: When set to true, forces the upsert to override existing content regardless of environment restrictions. This bypasses the development-only environment check and origin environment checks.

params.name: commit
params.description: Whether to commit the resource at the same time as modifying it.

params.name: commit_message
params.description: The message to commit the resource with, only used if `commit` is `true`.

items.docs:
Updates a message type, or creates a new one if it does not yet exist.

Note: this endpoint only operates in the `development` environment.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

params.name: branch
params.description: The slug of a branch to use. This option can only be used when `environment` is `"development"`.

params.name: annotate
params.description: Whether to annotate the resource. Only used in the Knock CLI.

params.name: hide_uncommitted_changes
params.description: Whether to hide uncommitted changes. When true, only committed changes will be returned. When false, both committed and uncommitted changes will be returned.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: limit
params.description: The number of entries to fetch per-page.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of message types available in a given environment.

params.name: environment
params.description: The environment slug.

items.docs: Given an authenticated service token and an environment, will exchange the service token for a secret API key that can be used to make requests to the public API.
